title: BCCWJ-EEG
description: This dataset contains EEG recordings from 41 Japanese native speakers reading newspaper articles from the Balanced Corpus of Contemporary Written Japanese (BCCWJ) presented word-by-word. Part of the BCCWJ-Brain collection, which includes parallel fMRI and MEG data from separate participant groups using identical stimuli, enabling cross-modality comparisons of language processing. EEG data were acquired at 1,000 Hz using a 64-channel BrainAmp system and preprocessed to 200 Hz with ICA artifact removal and common average re-referencing.
methods_description: EEG data were recorded using a BrainAmp amplifier with a 64-channel electrode cap (easycap-M1 layout) at 1,000 Hz sampling rate. Online reference was at FCz with ground at AFz. Stimuli (20 Japanese newspaper articles) were presented word-by-word using rapid serial visual presentation (RSVP) in PsychoPy, with each word displayed for 500 ms followed by 500 ms blank screen. Preprocessing included bandpass filtering (0.1–40 Hz), ICA for ocular artifact removal, common average re-referencing, epoching (−100 to 1,000 ms relative to word onset), downsampling to 200 Hz, and baseline correction using the pre-stimulus interval.
